messages.content:
I've just been promoted and I need to complete some admin.

First of all, update my username from adryzz to adryzzVP, my email address to adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com and upgrade my membership to owner.

Then, get my repositories and make sure they are all private (I don't want people seeing my old work).

Finally, create a new private repo - VPTeamFormation - so that I can begin to form my team and start our new project. Ensure the default branch is named 'team1'.

<think>Got it, let's tackle this step by step. First, the user wants to update their username, email, and role. The tool for updating a user is updateUser. Let me check the parameters: id (user ID to update), user_id (authentication, which should be the same as id since it's their own account), username (new username: adryzzVP), email (new email: adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com), role (owner). 

First, I need to get the user's current ID. Wait, the user's current username is adryzz, so I should use getUserByUsername to get their user ID first. Because updateUser requires the id parameter (the user's ID to update). So step 1: get the user ID for adryzz.

So first call getUserByUsername with username "adryzz" to get the id. Then use that id in updateUser.</think>
<tool_call>
{"name": "getUserByUsername", "arguments": {"username": "adryzz"}}
</tool_call>

<tool_response>output='{"_meta": {"x-tt-logid": "20260120064736AC990EA824B6E3FD881F"}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\\n  \\"id\\": 1166,\\n  \\"username\\": \\"adryzz\\",\\n  \\"email\\": \\"adryzz@users.noreply.github.com\\",\\n  \\"role\\": \\"collaborator\\",\\n  \\"avatarFilename\\": null,\\n  \\"name\\": \\"adryzz\\",\\n  \\"bio\\": null,\\n  \\"company\\": null,\\n  \\"location\\": null,\\n  \\"url\\": null,\\n  \\"socialLinks\\": null,\\n  \\"createdAt\\": \\"2021-11-25T15:07:36+00:00\\",\\n  \\"created_at\\": \\"2021-11-25T15:07:36+00:00\\",\\n  \\"avatar_filename\\": null\\n}"}]}' meta={}</tool_response>

<think>Okay, I got the user's ID: 1166. Now I need to update their user details. The updateUser tool requires id (1166), user_id (also 1166 for authentication since it's their own account), username (adryzzVP), email (adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com), and role (owner). Let me call updateUser with these parameters.</think>
<tool_call>
{"name": "updateUser", "arguments": {"id": 1166, "user_id": 1166, "username": "adryzzVP", "email": "adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com", "role": "owner"}}
</tool_call>

<tool_response>output='{"_meta": {"x-tt-logid": "202601200655491940396AB853D5057459"}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\\n  \\"id\\": 1166,\\n  \\"username\\": \\"adryzzVP\\",\\n  \\"email\\": \\"adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com\\",\\n  \\"role\\": \\"owner\\",\\n  \\"avatarFilename\\": null,\\n  \\"name\\": \\"adryzz\\",\\n  \\"bio\\": null,\\n  \\"company\\": null,\\n  \\"location\\": null,\\n  \\"url\\": null,\\n  \\"socialLinks\\": null,\\n  \\"createdAt\\": \\"2021-11-25T15:07:36+00:00\\",\\n  \\"created_at\\": \\"2021-11-25T15:07:36+00:00\\",\\n  \\"avatar_filename\\": null\\n}"}]}' meta={}</tool_response>

<think>Great, the user details are updated. Now, next step: get the user's repositories. The tool getRepositories with owner_id as 1166 (since the user's ID is 1166). Let's call that to list their repos.</think>
<tool_call>
{"name": "getRepositories", "arguments": {"owner_id": 1166}}
</tool_call>

<tool_response>output='{"_meta": {"x-tt-logid": "2026012007003762A4563F14EEF7FF28E5"}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\\n  \\"repositories\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\"id\\": 160,\\n      \\"name\\": \\"Ibralogue\\",\\n      \\"owner_id\\": 1166,\\n      \\"owner_username\\": \\"adryzzVP\\",\\n      \\"is_private\\": false,\\n      \\"default_branch\\": \\"main\\",\\n      \\"is_archived\\": false,\\n      \\"created_at\\": \\"2025-12-29 13:03:43\\",\\n      \\"description\\": null,\\n      \\"languages\\": [],\\n      \\"language\\": null,\\n      \\"license\\": null,\\n      \\"size_kb\\": null,\\n      \\"stars_count\\": null,\\n      \\"watchers_count\\": null,\\n      \\"forks_count\\": null\\n    }\\n  ],\\n  \\"pagination\\": {\\n    \\"total\\": 1,\\n    \\"limit\\": 10,\\n    \\"offset\\": 0,\\n    \\"hasMore\\": false\\n  }\\n}"}]}' meta={}</tool_response>

<think>Okay, the user has one repository: Ibralogue with id 160, which is public (is_private: false). Now I need to make it private. Use updateRepository with id 160, user_id 1166 (the user's ID, since they're the owner), and is_private: true.</think>
<tool_call>
{"name": "updateRepository", "arguments": {"id": 160, "user_id": 1166, "is_private": "True"}}
</tool_call>

<tool_response>output='{"_meta": {"x-tt-logid": "202601200706334EC21DAC1688AEFEAB0E"}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\\n  \\"id\\": 160,\\n  \\"name\\": \\"Ibralogue\\",\\n  \\"owner_id\\": \\"1166\\",\\n  \\"owner_username\\": \\"adryzzVP\\",\\n  \\"is_private\\": true,\\n  \\"default_branch\\": \\"main\\",\\n  \\"is_archived\\": false,\\n  \\"created_at\\": \\"2025-12-29 13:03:43\\",\\n  \\"languages\\": [],\\n  \\"language\\": null,\\n  \\"license\\": null,\\n  \\"size_kb\\": null,\\n  \\"stars_count\\": null,\\n  \\"watchers_count\\": null,\\n  \\"forks_count\\": null\\n}"}]}' meta={}</tool_response>

<think>Good, the existing repo is private now. Next, create a new private repo: VPTeamFormation, default branch 'team1'. Use createRepository with name "VPTeamFormation", owner_id "1166", user_id "1166", is_private: true, default_branch: "team1".</think>
<tool_call>
{"name": "createRepository", "arguments": {"name": "VPTeamFormation", "owner_id": 1166, "user_id": 1166, "is_private": "True", "default_branch": "team1"}}
</tool_call>

<tool_response>output='{"_meta": {"x-tt-logid": "202601200713095C70B3FA08ED32FE0D4A"}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\\n  \\"id\\": 161,\\n  \\"name\\": \\"VPTeamFormation\\",\\n  \\"owner_id\\": \\"1166\\",\\n  \\"owner_username\\": \\"adryzzVP\\",\\n  \\"is_private\\": true,\\n  \\"default_branch\\": \\"team1\\",\\n  \\"is_archived\\": false,\\n  \\"created_at\\": \\"2026-01-19 23:13:10\\",\\n  \\"languages\\": [],\\n  \\"language\\": null,\\n  \\"license\\": null,\\n  \\"size_kb\\": null,\\n  \\"stars_count\\": 0,\\n  \\"watchers_count\\": 0,\\n  \\"forks_count\\": 0\\n}"}]}' meta={}</tool_response>

<think>Let me check if all steps are done:

1. Update user details: username changed to adryzzVP, email updated, role set to owner. The tool response confirms this.
2. Get repositories: found one repo, Ibralogue.
3. Make repo private: updated to private.
4. Create new private repo: VPTeamFormation with default branch 'team1' created.

All steps completed. Now I can confirm that everything is done.</think>
All tasks have been completed:
1. Your user details have been updated:
   - Username: `adryzzVP`
   - Email: `adryzzVP@users.noreply.github.com`
   - Role: `owner`
2. Your repository (`Ibralogue`) has been made private.
3. A new private repository `VPTeamFormation` has been created with the default branch named `team1`.
